topic.title: Syntax Definition
topic.p: An AST definition consists of multiple rules. A @public can be used before any type definition to make it visible from another AST definition file.

EBNF is used in syntax definitions:
RuleOrTokenName:FieldName: Match the rule or token and store the result to the field. The class type of this rule can be either specified or inferred.
!RuleName: The whole rule will modify fields in the same object from RuleName instead of creating a new object.
(Syntax): Match Syntax, just for fixing operator priority.
[Syntax]: Match Syntax optionally.
+[Syntax]: Match Syntax optionally, when ambiguity occurs, the opt-in one wins.
SyntaxA SyntaxB: Match SyntaxA followed by SyntaxB.
SyntaxA | SyntaxB: Match either SyntaxA or SyntaxB.
{Syntax}: Match zero or more occurrences of Syntax.
{SyntaxA ; SyntaxB}: Match zero or more occurrences of SyntaxA separating by SyntaxB. This is a shortcut for [SyntaxA {SyntaxB SyntaxA}].
